data.title: Is Airbus (ENXTPA:AIR) Still Attractive After Strong Multi Year Share Price Gains
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Airbus is fairly priced at around €215.10, or if recent enthusiasm has pushed it ahead of its fundamentals, this article is for you.
  • Airbus shares recently closed at €215.10, with returns of 3.4% over 7 days, 10.9% over 30 days, 5.6% year to date, 41.5% over 1 year, 96.8% over 3 years and 152.4% over 5 years. These figures raise a clear question about what is already reflected in the price.
  • Recent attention on Airbus has focused on its position in global commercial aircraft supply and continued interest in its order pipeline from airlines and leasing companies. This context has helped frame the current share price, as investors weigh long term demand for air travel and fleet renewal against industry wide capacity and supply considerations.
  • Our valuation checks currently give Airbus a 5 out of 6 score for potential undervaluation. Next, we will walk through the key valuation approaches, before finishing with a way of looking at value that can give you an even clearer long term picture.

Find out why Airbus's 41.5% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Airbus Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model takes estimates of the cash a company may generate in the future and discounts each year back to today, to arrive at an estimate of what the entire business could be worth right now.

For Airbus, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about €3.8b. Analysts provide detailed forecasts for the next few years, and Simply Wall St then extends those estimates further, giving projected free cash flow of €11.0b by 2030. The values between those years reflect both analyst inputs and extrapolated figures.

Bringing all of these projected cash flows back to today and adding them up produces an estimated intrinsic value of about €282.29 per share. Compared with the recent share price of €215.10, this implies a 23.8% discount, which points to the shares trading below this DCF estimate.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Airbus is undervalued by 23.8%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 883 more undervalued stocks based on cash flows.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Airbus.

Approach 2: Airbus Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies like Airbus, the P/E ratio is a common way to think about value, because it links what you pay directly to the earnings the business is currently generating. Higher growth expectations or lower perceived risk usually justify a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk often point to a lower, more cautious multiple.

Airbus currently trades on a P/E of 33.5x. That sits below the Aerospace & Defense industry average of about 51.5x and also below the peer average of 38.9x. On the surface, that suggests the market is applying a lower multiple than many comparable names in the sector.

Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Airbus is 34.9x, which is its view of what a reasonable P/E could be given the company’s earnings growth profile, profit margins, industry, market value and risk factors. This Fair Ratio can be more useful than a simple peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for Airbus’ specific characteristics rather than assuming all companies deserve the same multiple. With the current P/E at 33.5x versus a Fair Ratio of 34.9x, Airbus shares screen as slightly undervalued on this metric.

Result: UNDERVALUED

data.title: A Piece Of The Puzzle Missing From S.C. IOR S.A.'s (BVB:IORB) Share Price
data.description:
It's not a stretch to say that S.C. IOR S.A.'s (BVB:IORB) price-to-sales (or "P/S") ratio of 2.8x right now seems quite "middle-of-the-road" for companies in the Market industry in Romania, where the median P/S ratio is around 3.3x. However, investors might be overlooking a clear opportunity or potential setback if there is no rational basis for the P/S.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Advertisement

What Does S.C. IOR's Recent Performance Look Like?

Recent times have been quite advantageous for S.C. IOR as its revenue has been rising very briskly. Perhaps the market is expecting future revenue performance to taper off, which has kept the P/S from rising. If that doesn't eventuate, then existing shareholders have reason to be feeling optimistic about the future direction of the share price.

Want the full picture on earnings, revenue and cash flow for the company? Then our free report on S.C. IOR will help you shine a light on its historical performance.

Is There Some Revenue Growth Forecasted For S.C. IOR?

S.C. IOR's P/S ratio would be typical for a company that's only expected to deliver moderate growth, and importantly, perform in line with the industry.

If we review the last year of revenue growth, the company posted a terrific increase of 194%. The latest three year period has also seen an excellent 236% overall rise in revenue, aided by its short-term performance. Accordingly, shareholders would have definitely welcomed those medium-term rates of revenue growth.

This is in contrast to the rest of the industry, which is expected to grow by 6.3% over the next year, materially lower than the company's recent medium-term annualised growth rates.

In light of this, it's curious that S.C. IOR's P/S sits in line with the majority of other companies. Apparently some shareholders believe the recent performance is at its limits and have been accepting lower selling prices.

What We Can Learn From S.C. IOR's P/S?

Typically, we'd caution against reading too much into price-to-sales ratios when settling on investment decisions, though it can reveal plenty about what other market participants think about the company.

To our surprise, S.C. IOR revealed its three-year revenue trends aren't contributing to its P/S as much as we would have predicted, given they look better than current industry expectations. It'd be fair to assume that potential risks the company faces could be the contributing factor to the lower than expected P/S. While recent revenue trends over the past medium-term suggest that the risk of a price decline is low, investors appear to see the likelihood of revenue fluctuations in the future.

It is also worth noting that we have found 3 warning signs for S.C. IOR (1 is a bit unpleasant!) that you need to take into consideration.

data.description:

(BMV:Q) may be sending bullish signals at the moment, given that almost half of all companies in Mexico have P/E ratios greater than 14x and even P/E's higher than 22x are not unusual. Although, it's not wise to just take the P/E at face value as there may be an explanation why it's limited.

With earnings growth that's superior to most other companies of late, Quálitas Controladora. de has been doing relatively well. If you like the company, you'd be hoping this isn't the case so that you could potentially pick up some stock while it's out of favour.

Advertisement

How Is Quálitas Controladora. de's Growth Trending?

In order to justify its P/E ratio, Quálitas Controladora. de would need to produce sluggish growth that's trailing the market.

Retrospectively, the last year delivered an exceptional 36% gain to the company's bottom line. The latest three year period has also seen an excellent 175% overall rise in EPS, aided by its short-term performance. Therefore, it's fair to say the earnings growth recently has been superb for the company.

Looking ahead now, EPS is anticipated to climb by 3.6% each year during the coming three years according to the nine analysts following the company. Meanwhile, the rest of the market is forecast to expand by 12% each year, which is noticeably more attractive.

With this information, we can see why Quálitas Controladora. de is trading at a P/E lower than the market. Apparently many shareholders weren't comfortable holding on while the company is potentially eyeing a less prosperous future.

The Key Takeaway

Using the price-to-earnings ratio alone to determine if you should sell your stock isn't sensible, however it can be a practical guide to the company's future prospects.

We've established that Quálitas Controladora. de maintains its low P/E on the weakness of its forecast growth being lower than the wider market, as expected. Right now shareholders are accepting the low P/E as they concede future earnings probably won't provide any pleasant surprises. data.title: Latest News In Digital Payment - Euronet Expands Through Strategic CrediaBank Partnership
data.description:
Euronet Worldwide, Inc. has entered into a strategic partnership agreement with Greece's CrediaBank to acquire its merchant acquiring business, signaling significant developments in the digital payment sector. This transaction, anticipated to close in the third quarter of 2026 pending regulatory approvals, will consolidate Euronet's standing in Greek merchant services by merging with CrediaBank's portfolio. Additionally, the collaboration includes the provision of financial services to CrediaBank and the launch of an account-to-account consumer digital wallet, positioning both companies to meet the growing demand for digital and cardless payment solutions. This partnership is set to enhance customer service reach and expand digital offerings, contributing to the broader evolution of the digital payment landscape in Europe.

Elsewhere in the market, QNB Bank (IBSE:QNBTR) was a notable mover up 10% and ending trading at TRY492.25.
